title: alsine × alpestris
intro: Alsine × alpestris, also known as alpine chickweed, is a rare hybrid plant that belongs to the pink family (Caryophyllaceae) and is a cross between Alsine media and Alchemilla alpestris. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Alsine × alpestris include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and even alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Herbal preparations of Alsine × alpestris are typically made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
